title: McNemar test uses incorrect hybrid min(b,c) + two-sided binomtest
description: binomtest(min(b, c), n_discordant, 0.5) combines min() with default two-sided test, producing overly conservative p-values. Should use binomtest(b, n_discordant, 0.5, alternative='two-sided') or binomtest(min(b,c), n_discordant, 0.5, alternative='greater').

title: Embedding normalization produces NaN on zero-norm vectors
description: Division by zero L2 norm produces NaN that silently propagates through all downstream cosine similarity calculations. Can happen with empty or very short input strings.

title: Cosine similarity assertion fails on floating-point imprecision
description: assert -1.0 <= score <= 1.0 can fail due to floating-point imprecision in dot products of L2-normalized vectors (e.g., 1.0000000149). Would halt the entire experiment pipeline.

title: target_sets not schema-validated after JSON load
description: target_sets loaded from JSON as generic dicts. Missing keys (id, text, granularity, role/roles) only surface deep in pipeline at encoding time, not at load time.

title: Descriptions module doesn't validate LLM returned all roles
description: After parsing Claude API JSON response for role descriptions, code doesn't check that all roles in the category received descriptions. Missing roles cause KeyError downstream in build_target_sets.

title: validate_cases doesn't check category consistency with taxonomy
description: Validation checks role name exists in taxonomy but not that the category field matches the taxonomy's category for that role. Incorrect category silently causes evaluation errors.

title: best_emb selection is alphabetical, not data-driven
description: run_statistical_tests picks first alphabetically-sorted embedding model for pairwise McNemar tests against baselines. Statistical significance claims may not reflect the best embedding model's advantage.

title: Dev sanity check uses first model, not best performer
description: run_experiment.py uses config['models'][0] for dev check, not the best-performing model. Dead assignment dev_rankings = [] on line 69 is immediately overwritten.

title: Friedman+Nemenyi tests not implemented but documented
description: Project documentation mentions Friedman+Nemenyi for multi-method comparisons but statistics module only implements McNemar pairwise. Either implement or update documentation.

title: _ABBREVIATIONS and _MINOR_REWORDS overlap reduces test diversity
description: Both transform dictionaries map many of the same keys to nearly identical abbreviations (e.g., Manager->Mgr vs Manager->Mgr). Produces near-duplicate test cases that low-power Jaccard dedup won't catch.

title: np.load without allow_pickle=False in embed.py
description: np.load(cache_path) without allow_pickle=False. If cache directory is writable (e.g., on shared garden-pop), a malicious .npy could execute arbitrary code. Numpy >= 1.16.3 defaults to False but explicit is safer.

title: HuggingFace model loaded without integrity verification docs
description: sentence-transformers loads model code from HuggingFace Hub. Revision hashes are pinned (good), but no documentation that hashes were manually verified. trust_remote_code not explicitly disabled.

title: Unclosed file handle in prep_taxonomy.py
description: yaml.safe_load(open('config.yaml')) opens file without context manager. Inconsistent with other scripts that use 'with' statements.

title: Assertions used for runtime validation should be proper exceptions
description: Critical validations in embed.py and baselines.py use assert statements that are stripped with -O flag. Should use if/raise ValueError for data validation.

title: Bootstrap closures rebuild case_lookup per ranking in inner loop
description: case_lookup dict rebuilt inside per-ranking loop in _make_mrr_fn and _make_topk_fn. O(n^2) per resample. Move outside inner loop.

title: Code fence stripping inconsistency between descriptions.py and generate_llm.py
description: Two different approaches to stripping code fences from LLM responses. Should be a shared utility for consistency.

title: report.py n_roles wraps single value in unnecessary list comprehension
description: sum(len(ts) for ts in [target_sets.get('role', [])]) is equivalent to len(target_sets.get('role', [])). Misleading comprehension.

title: prep_test_data.py hardcodes manual.json path
description: manual_cases path hardcoded instead of coming from config.yaml like other paths.

title: test_taxonomy.py depends on real taxonomy file (integration test)
description: test_build_clusters calls parse_taxonomy('job-roles.md') using relative path to real data. Will fail if working directory changes.

title: Dead variable dev_fraction in validate.py
description: dev_fraction = dev_size / len(cases) computed but never used.

title: callable type hint should be collections.abc.Callable
description: Lowercase callable used as type hint in generate_rules.py. Should be typing.Callable or collections.abc.Callable.

title: Duplicate load_json/save_json across orchestrator scripts
description: All three scripts define identical load_json/save_json helpers. Should be a shared utility.

title: No validation of config values used in cache path construction
description: model_label from config.yaml used in os.path.join for cache paths without validation. Low risk since config is developer-controlled.

title: LLM response data used without field-level schema validation
description: JSON parsed from Claude API responses only checked for being dict/list, not individual field presence or types. Missing fields produce unhelpful KeyErrors.

title: Error messages include unbounded raw LLM response content
description: descriptions.py includes full response_text in ValueError. generate_llm.py truncates to 500 chars. Should be consistent and truncated.

title: results/ directory not in .gitignore
description: Results directory with full ranking data and error analysis not excluded from version control.

title: generate_rule_cases could benefit from transform failure logging
description: When a transform+role combination fails to produce output, the loop silently falls through to fallback pool. Logging would aid debugging.

resolution: Changed to binomtest(b, n_discordant, 0.5, alternative='two-sided') — proper two-sided test without min()
Added zero-norm check before division in encode_targets and encode_queries, raises ValueError
Added np.clip(scores, -1.0, 1.0) before range check, replaced assert with raise ValueError
Added validate_target_sets() function checking required keys per granularity
Added post-parse validation that all roles received descriptions
Added category consistency check against taxonomy lookup
Replaced alphabetical first with max MRR selection via _compute_mrr helper
Removed dead assignment, renamed best_model to dev_model, added clarifying comment
Implemented friedman_nemenyi_test() with tie-aware ranking, scipy friedmanchisquare, and Nemenyi CD
Replaced _MINOR_REWORDS with distinct synonyms (Lead, Developer, Expert, etc.), fixed Admin duplicate
Added allow_pickle=False to np.load call
Added trust_remote_code=False and docstring about pinned revision hashes
Replaced inline open() with context manager, now uses src.utils.save_json
Replaced all assert statements with if/raise ValueError in embed.py and baselines.py
Moved case_lookup construction before inner ranking loop in bootstrap closures
Added consistent _strip_code_fences() function to both descriptions.py and generate_llm.py
Simplified to len(target_sets.get('role', []))
Path now constructed from config test_data.output_dir
Added @pytest.mark.skipif for missing taxonomy file
Removed unused dev_fraction variable
Changed to collections.abc.Callable
Created src/utils.py, all 3 scripts import from it
Added path separator validation for model_label
Added required-key validation in all three LLM pass functions
Truncated response_text to 500 chars in error message
Added results/ and !results/.gitkeep to .gitignore
Added per-failure detail logging (transform:role) with aggregate count
